About ISM

Independent Study & Mentorship: sustained inquiry, real-world learning, and a student-built project.

Independent Study & Mentorship (ISM) is a year-long course that allows students to explore a career field through research, professional interaction, and a long-term project. Students begin by studying their selected industry, building foundational knowledge through research assessments, interviews, mentor visits, and observation of real workplaces and practices.

As the year progresses, students apply what they learn by proposing and developing an original project aligned to their field. The ISM process emphasizes sustained inquiry: asking focused questions, gathering and interpreting information, and refining ideas through reflection and iteration. The end result is a professional digital portfolio that documents learning, growth, and an authentic project.

What this portfolio shows

  • My topic of study and professional goals
  • Research and reflections collected throughout the year
  • Evidence of professional engagement (mentor work, interviews, observations)
  • Original Work and the progression toward my Final Product
=======

Program Context

About ISM

Independent Study and Mentorship program context.

How ISM structures research, professional engagement, and a long-cycle original project.

Updated: March 2026

What ISM Is

Independent Study and Mentorship (ISM) is a year-long course where students investigate a career field through structured research, professional interaction, and a major project.

Students build foundational understanding through assessments, interviews, mentor observations, and field-specific documentation. They then convert that learning into an original work product and final portfolio evidence.

What This Portfolio Demonstrates

  • Topic selection and technical career intent.
  • Progressive research artifacts and reflection quality.
  • Professional engagement through mentor and industry interaction.
  • Original Work progression toward final product completion.
